summary.goal: Implement a CLI subcommand for LLM-assisted node summary drafting
decisions: Used reqwest with blocking feature for synchronous code
Constructed the LLM endpoint from the `base_url` in .llmgraph/config.toml
Instructed the model to return only a raw JSON object and handled potential markdown wrapping
Opened the draft in the user's `$EDITOR` for review and correction

rejected_approaches.description: Using an async reqwest client with tokio
rejected_approaches.reason: Would have required adding tokio as a dependency and wrapping main with #[tokio::main], which is heavier than needed for a synchronous CLI.
Adds complexity for no UX benefit since we open an editor afterward anyway.
